observations.value: POST /session with any non-empty credentials (e.g. identifier=x, secret=y or identifier=wrong@example.com, secret=wrongpassword) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a 'Welcome' home page instead of HTTP 401 with an error — the login endpoint accepts any non-empty credentials as valid, bypassing authentication entirely.
POST /session with identifier=admin, secret=hunter2 (arbitrary credentials) returns HTTP 200 OK with Set-Cookie: session=ok, confirming no credential validation is performed whatsoever.

GET /logout returns HTTP 404 Not Found, meaning there is no logout endpoint accessible via GET; the page at /login shows 'Sign out' linking to /logout but neither GET nor POST /logout works (both return 404), so users cannot sign out.
POST /cart/checkout (the 'Proceed to checkout' action) returns HTTP 200 with the Payment page without requiring any authentication — no login or session cookie is needed to reach the payment step.

POST /order with card=4111111111111111 returns HTTP 200 'Order confirmed' without any session or authentication cookie required — the order placement endpoint is fully unauthenticated and accepts orders without login.
